title: Train FOOD Act
stage_label: Referred to the Subcommittee on Railroads, Pipelines, and Hazardous Materials.
plain_summary: The Train FOOD Act tells Amtrak to create a special group to help improve the food and drinks served on trains. This group must write a report every year about how well Amtrak is doing at making the food service better and share it with Congress and on Amtrak's website. The law also requires another government office to check on Amtrak's progress after two years and write their own report. The goal is to make sure train passengers get better food and drink options when they travel.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Railroads, Pipelines, and Hazardous Materials.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Train Furtherance of Outstanding Onboard Dining Act or the Train FOOD Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Oversight of Implementation by Amtrak of Recommendations of Amtrak Food and Beverage Working Group
sections.text: This section changes the law to require Amtrak to create an advisory committee and submit yearly reports about improving food and drinks on trains. Within one year after this law passes, Amtrak must set up an advisory committee to review how well they are following recommendations from an earlier report about food and beverage service. This committee will include people from Amtrak, unions that represent workers who prepare and serve food on trains, groups that speak for train passengers, and states that help pay for certain train routes. The committee will end when Amtrak finishes its final report. Starting one year after this law passes and every year after that, Amtrak must send a report to two congressional committees and post it on their website. The report must continue until all recommendations are either completed or Amtrak decides they cannot be done. Each yearly report must describe what progress Amtrak has made on each recommendation, list which recommendations are finished, identify recommendations that Amtrak thinks are too hard or impossible to do, explain why those recommendations cannot be done and estimate costs if the problem is lack of money, describe how food and beverage service has changed since the law passed or since the last report, and include comments from the advisory committee about all this information. Additionally, within two years after this law passes, the Government Accountability Office (an independent agency that investigates government spending and operations) must send a report to the same congressional committees describing Amtrak's progress on implementing the food and beverage recommendations.
